title: LoopActive — the loop-active marker (the I-9 arming-gap signal gate-budget reads)
description: Persisted at .harness/run/loop-active.json by lattice.loop_marker_set as step 0a of /harness-run, BEFORE the budget is armed (step 0b). Its presence means an autonomous loop is running; while it is set and no run budget is armed, gate-budget denies every worker write (the arming gap, fail-closed). A marker older than lattice.LOOP_TTL_S is treated as STALE (a crashed run's corpse) so a crash can't wedge the project deny-closed forever. Typed and check-gated like its sibling run-budget.json (the durable .harness/run/ state must not be a representable-but-illegal shape). See bin/lattice.py (loop_marker_*), bin/gate-budget, and bin/run-budget.py (mark/stop).

active.description: Must be true — the marker's presence IS the signal that a loop is active; the field is a redundant guard. A marker file with active:false is an illegal state.

started_ts.description: ISO-8601 timestamp (with offset) when the loop was marked. Used to detect a stale (crashed) marker via LOOP_TTL_S.

label.description: A human label for the run (e.g. the /harness-run arguments). Diagnostic only.

pid.description: PID of the process that wrote the marker. Diagnostic only (the writer is a short-lived CLI, so PID liveness is not used for staleness — age is).
